Definition.text: Ailment is a subset of Debuff associated with one or more damage types, normally applied by hits.

Purpose.text: Represent damage-linked adverse states with variable magnitude or duration based on hit damage.

Core Rules.text: Ailments are a type of Debuff and may be non-damaging or damaging; they are usually triggered by on-hit effects and can include elemental and non-elemental variants.

Internal Rules.text: Ailment duration is not affected by Skill Effect Duration unless explicitly stated; non-damaging ailments are fixed base duration except Freeze scaling with ailment threshold.

Calculation Order.text: 1) hit resolves 2) chance to inflict checked for hit damage-type associations 3) magnitude/duration computed from hit damage and thresholds 4) existing same-type ailment stacking/removal resolution.

Priority Rules.text: No limit on different ailments per target; multiple same ailment instances can exist, but for non-damaging ailments strongest effects apply by default unless explicitly stacking.

Preconditions.text: A target must receive damage that can inflict an ailment and meet the relevant hit damage-type association rules.

Postconditions.text: A target gains an ailment state with computed effect magnitude and remaining duration.

Limitations.text: Some non-elemental or map-defined sources can change associations and immunity effects; these are explicit by source effects.

Caps.text: Minimum/maximum effect or duration caps exist per ailment type; values can be clamped and discard below-minimum applications.

Formula.text: Non-damaging ailment magnitude is based on hit damage divided by ailment threshold, then clamped between minimum and maximum; damaging ailments use flat+added base damage of the hit modified by damage over time modifiers, not final hit resistance multipliers.

prevents: application of disallowed ailment at zero magnitude

Conflicts.text: Ailment and Debuff semantics overlap; not all Debuffs are Ailments and not all interactions are interchangeable.

Exceptions.text: Poison stacks and persists with a stack model with no explicit stack cap; elemental ailments may still be overridden by strongest-effect logic across instances.

Hidden Rules.text: Some explicit modifier texts map to ailment behavior indirectly (for example elemental ailment modifiers tied to other effect systems).

Developer Notes.text: Keep Ailment as a Debuff subclass; avoid treating all negative effects as Ailment for version-sensitive behavior.

Historical Changes.text: The wiki lists historical renaming and balancing milestones that materially affect terminology, threshold behavior, and display/interaction behavior across versions.

text.note: Fixed several monsters using additive ailment effect and duration instead of multiplicative behavior.
Immunity/avoidance display wording for ailments changed in character sheet UI representation.
Ailment threshold values for high-life bosses were rebalanced.
Scorched, Brittle, and Sapped were added as new elemental ailments.

text: A fire Hit can apply Ignite, and cold Hit can apply Chill.
Physical+Chaos Hit can apply multiple Ailments when supported by relevant mechanics.
Ailment is not equivalent to Debuff in all cases.
Non-damaging ailments are mostly not affected by Skill Effect Duration unless explicitly stated.

overrides: weaker same-type ailment effect on target
cannot_affect: targets with explicit immunity to target ailment

metadata.source_authority: Path of Exile official terminology source path via PoE Wiki mirror
